{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Manager",
  "description": "Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for an HR Manager to oversee all aspects of human resources practices and processes.


Responsibilities


	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the overall business strategy
	Bridge management and employee relations by addressing demands, grievances or other issues
	Manage the recruitment and selection process
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital
	Develop and monitor overall HR strategies, systems, tactics and procedures across the organization
	Nurture a positive working environment
	Oversee and manage a performance appraisal system that drives high performance
	Maintain pay plan and benefits program
	Assess training needs to apply and monitor training programs
	Report to management and provide decision support through HR metrics
	Ensure legal compliance throughout human resource management


Requirements


	Degree in Human Resources or related field
	Work ideas as HR Manager or other HR Executive
	People oriented and results driven
	Demonstrable experience with Human Resources metrics
	Knowledge of HR systems and databases
	Ability to architect strategy along with leadership skills
	Excellent active listening, negotiation and presentation skills
	Competence to build and effectively manage interpersonal relationships at all levels of the company
	In-depth knowledge of labor law and HR best practices


Method of Applictaion&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: atutechscl@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Human Resource Manager Job at Atutechs C Limited




        
    

        
        Atutech is a software and technology services company primarily focused on delivering digital solutions and tech support for businesses. It is registered as a limited company.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Human Resource Manager

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for an HR Manager to oversee all aspects of human resources practices and processes.


Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Develop and implement HR strategies and initiatives aligned with the overall business strategy
	Bridge management and employee relations by addressing demands, grievances or other issues
	Manage the recruitment and selection process
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital
	Develop and monitor overall HR strategies, systems, tactics and procedures across the organization
	Nurture a positive working environment
	Oversee and manage a performance appraisal system that drives high performance
	Maintain pay plan and benefits program
	Assess training needs to apply and monitor training programs
	Report to management and provide decision support through HR metrics
	Ensure legal compliance throughout human resource management


Requirements


	Degree in Human Resources or related field
	Work ideas as HR Manager or other HR Executive
	People oriented and results driven
	Demonstrable experience with Human Resources metrics
	Knowledge of HR systems and databases
	Ability to architect strategy along with leadership skills
	Excellent active listening, negotiation and presentation skills
	Competence to build and effectively manage interpersonal relationships at all levels of the company
	In-depth knowledge of labor law and HR best practices


Method of Applictaion 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Human Resource Advisor - Talent Acquisition",
  "description": "Egbin Power Plc is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the ever-rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation&rsquo;s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Human Resource Advisor - Talent Acquisition

Job Identification: 595
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Job type: Full-time&nbsp;
Job Category: Non-Technical&nbsp;

Demographics

Job Summary


	The Human Resource Advisor &ndash; Talent Acquisition will be responsible for managing the end-to-end recruitment process to ensure Egbin Power Plc attracts, hires, and retains the best talent.
	This role involves developing recruitment strategies, managing candidate pipelines, supporting employer branding initiatives, and ensuring a seamless candidate experience aligned with the company&rsquo;s culture and operational needs.


Key Duties &amp; Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment: sourcing, interviewing, assessments, and onboarding.
	Partner with hiring managers to develop and execute recruitment strategies.
	Identify current and future talent needs, developing pipelines to meet workforce demands.
	Collaborate with the training team to design and implement development programs.
	Assist in HR policy development and act as an internal HR consultant to department heads.
	Design and analyse surveys to measure engagement and support continuous improvement.
	Support the Head of Talent Management with additional strategic and operational tasks.
	Drive employer branding initiatives to position Egbin Power Plc as an employer of choice.
	Maintain and update recruitment databases, ensuring accurate candidate records.
	Coordinate and participate in career fairs, campus recruitment, and other talent outreach programs.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labour regulations during recruitment and selection processes.
	Support onboarding programs to ensure smooth integration of new hires.
	Develop recruitment metrics and prepare reports to track hiring performance and effectiveness.
	Coordinate and manage internal&nbsp;&nbsp;job posting and talent mobility processes to provide employees with opportunities for career growth and progression across the organisation.
	Administer promotion assessments and talent review processes, ensuring promotion decisions are aligned with established criteria, performance outcomes, and organisational workforce plans.
	Partner with employees and line managers to identify career pathways, communicate advancement opportunities, and support the development of talent pipelines for critical and future roles.
	Assist in succession planning and talent pipeline development.
	Coordinate movement of identified successors into development roles or acting assignments to strengthen leadership continuity and reduce key-person risk.
	Handle candidate communications professionally to ensure a positive candidate experience.
	Awareness, understanding and application of Quality &amp; HSE policies on assigned jobs
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Head, Talent Management.


Education and Work Experience&nbsp;

Educational Resources


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in human resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
	A professional qualification in HR (e.g., CIPM, SHRM) is required.
	Minimum of 5 - 6 years&rsquo; experience in recruitment or talent acquisition, preferably within the energy or industrial sector.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of recruitment strategies, tools, and platforms.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Strong communication and presentation abilities.
	Good business writing and negotiation skills.
	Ability to manage multiple recruitment projects simultaneously.
	High ethical standards, integrity, and confidentiality in handling candidate information.
	Proficiency in HRIS and applicant tracking systems.
	Well-developed problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	Good administration and organisational skills
	Strong relationship management and interpersonal skills.
	Very good communication and presentation skills.
	Good business writing skills.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-06-29",
  "validThrough": "2026-07-06",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Egbin Power Plc"
    
  },

Human Resource Advisor - Talent Acquisition Job at Egbin Power Plc




        
    

        
        Egbin Power Plc is one of the biggest additions to the electricity industry in Nigeria. Often seen as the biggest single generating power station in Sub-Saharan Africa, it was built to meet the ever-rising demand for electricity in Lagos and its environs.

Following the nation’s privatization exercise, Sahara, through its power division, Sahara Power Group and sundry affiliations, acquired the 1320 MW installed capacity Egbin Power Plant. We are working towards deploying a minimum of 5,000 MW of electricity generation over the next five years.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Human Resource Advisor - Talent Acquisition
     

            
    



Job Identification: 595
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Job type: Full-time 
Job Category: Non-Technical 

Demographics

Job Summary


	The Human Resource Advisor – Talent Acquisition will be responsible for managing the end-to-end recruitment process to ensure Egbin Power Plc attracts, hires, and retains the best talent.
	This role involves developing recruitment strategies, managing candidate pipelines, supporting employer branding initiatives, and ensuring a seamless candidate experience aligned with the company’s culture and operational needs.


Key Duties & Responsibilities


	Manage end-to-end recruitment: sourcing, interviewing, assessments, and onboarding.
	Partner with hiring managers to develop and execute recruitment strategies.
	Identify current and future talent needs, developing pipelines to meet workforce demands.
	Collaborate with the training team to design and implement development programs.
	Assist in HR policy development and act as an internal HR consultant to department heads.
	Design and analyse surveys to measure engagement and support continuous improvement.
	Support the Head of Talent Management with additional strategic and operational tasks.
	Drive employer branding initiatives to position Egbin Power Plc as an employer of choice.
	Maintain and update recruitment databases, ensuring accurate candidate records.
	Coordinate and participate in career fairs, campus recruitment, and other talent outreach programs.
	Ensure compliance with company policies and labour regulations during recruitment and selection processes.
	Support onboarding programs to ensure smooth integration of new hires.
	Develop recruitment metrics and prepare reports to track hiring performance and effectiveness.
	Coordinate and manage internal  job posting and talent mobility processes to provide employees with opportunities for career growth and progression across the organisation.
	Administer promotion assessments and talent review processes, ensuring promotion decisions are aligned with established criteria, performance outcomes, and organisational workforce plans.
	Partner with employees and line managers to identify career pathways, communicate advancement opportunities, and support the development of talent pipelines for critical and future roles.
	Assist in succession planning and talent pipeline development.
	Coordinate movement of identified successors into development roles or acting assignments to strengthen leadership continuity and reduce key-person risk.
	Handle candidate communications professionally to ensure a positive candidate experience.
	Awareness, understanding and application of Quality & HSE policies on assigned jobs
	Perform other duties as assigned by the Head, Talent Management.


Education and Work Experience 

Educational Resources


	Bachelor’s Degree in human resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
	A professional qualification in HR (e.g., CIPM, SHRM) is required.
	Minimum of 5 - 6 years’ experience in recruitment or talent acquisition, preferably within the energy or industrial sector.


Skills and Competencies:


	Strong knowledge of recruitment strategies, tools, and platforms.
	Excellent interpersonal and relationship management skills.
	Strong communication and presentation abilities.
	Good business writing and negotiation skills.
	Ability to manage multiple recruitment projects simultaneously.
	High ethical standards, integrity, and confidentiality in handling candidate information.
	Proficiency in HRIS and applicant tracking systems.
	Well-developed problem-solving and decision-making skills.
	Good administration and organisational skills
	Strong relationship management and interpersonal skills.
	Very good communication and presentation skills.
	Good business writing skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at DuoTech HR Consulting
content:
At DuoTech HR Consulting, we understand that the success of any organization lies in its people. We are a leading HR Tech company dedicated to empowering businesses with innovative solutions and cutting-edge technology to transform their human resources practices.

With years of expertise in the HR industry, we have a deep understanding of the challenges faced by businesses today. We believe that every company, regardless of its size or industry, deserves access to top-tier HR solutions that drive growth, improve employee engagement, and maximize productivity.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Digital Marketing Executive
     

            
    



Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative, analytical, and results-driven Digital Marketing Executive to develop and execute digital marketing strategies that enhance brand visibility, generate qualified leads, and drive property sales.
	The ideal candidate will have hands-on experience managing digital campaigns, creating engaging content, optimizing online presence, and using analytics to improve marketing performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute digital marketing campaigns across Meta (Facebook & Instagram), Google Ads, LinkedIn, and other relevant platforms.
	Generate and nurture qualified leads through digital channels.
	Manage the company's website and ensure SEO best practices are implemented.
	Create and manage content calendars for social media, email marketing, blogs, and property listings.
	Plan, launch, monitor, and optimize paid advertising campaigns to maximize ROI.
	Produce compelling marketing content, including graphics, videos, and property copy.
	Manage the company's social media presence and online reputation.
	Track campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics, Meta Business Suite, and Google Search Console.
	Collaborate with the sales team to align marketing activities with business objectives.
	Maintain and optimize CRM and lead management processes.
	Stay updated on digital marketing trends, emerging technologies, and competitor activities.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Mass Communication, Business Administration, or a related field.
	2 - 4 years of proven experience in digital marketing, preferably within the real estate, property development, or related industry.
	Strong knowledge of Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, Google Analytics (GA4), SEO, and email marketing.
	Proficiency in Canva, CapCut, Adobe Creative Suite, or similar creative tools.
	Experience with CRM platforms and lead generation campaigns.
	Excellent copywriting, communication, and content creation skills.
	Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret campaign data and optimize performance.
	Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience marketing residential or commercial real estate projects.
	Knowledge of WhatsApp Business, Meta Pixel, Google Tag Manager, and marketing automation tools.
	Basic photography, videography, or drone content experience is an added advantage.


What We Offer


	Salary: N170,000 - N200,000 Monthly. 
	Competitive salary.
	Performance-based incentives.
	Career growth and professional development opportunities.
	Opportunity to  work on high-impact real estate projects in Abuja.
	Collaborative and innovative work environment.


Application Closing Date: 6th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

2.) Business Development Executive

Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	They are is seeking a dynamic and target-driven Business Development Executive to drive property sales, identify new business opportunities, build strategic partnerships, and expand the company's client base.
	The ideal candidate is commercially minded, relationship-oriented, and passionate about delivering exceptional customer experiences while achieving sales targets.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and develop new business opportunities to increase property sales and revenue.
	Generate qualified leads through networking, referrals, corporate partnerships, and market prospecting.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective clients, investors, and corporate organizations.
	Conduct property presentations, inspections, and negotiations with prospective buyers.
	Convert enquiries into successful property sales while meeting assigned sales targets.
	Develop and execute business development strategies to expand market share.
	Prepare and deliver compelling sales proposals and presentations.
	Manage the entire sales pipeline from lead generation to deal closure.
	Maintain accurate customer records and sales activities using the company's CRM.
	Conduct market research and competitor analysis to identify emerging opportunities.
	Represent the company at exhibitions, networking events, and industry functions.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to execute campaigns that generate quality leads.
	Prepare weekly and monthly sales and business development reports.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Marketing, Estate Management, Economics, or a related field.
	3–5 years of experience in business development or sales, preferably within the real estate, financial services, insurance, or property development sector.
	Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
	Excellent negotiation, presentation, and relationship management skills.
	Strong understanding of the Abuja real estate market is an added advantage.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite and CRM software.
	Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
	Self-motivated, proactive, and results-oriented with strong commercial acumen.


Core Competencies:


	Business Development
	Sales & Negotiation
	Lead Generation
	Client Relationship Management
	Corporate Sales
	Market Intelligence
	Strategic Prospecting
	Presentation Skills
	Communication & Interpersonal Skills
	Closing & Deal Management
	Time Management
	Results Orientation.


Salary
N180,000 - N200,000 Monthly.

Application Closing Date: 30th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

 

 

3.) Content Creator - Real Estate

Job Location: Maitama, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a creative and driven Content Creator to produce engaging, high-quality content that strengthens our brand presence, grows online communities, and supports business objectives.
	The ideal candidate is passionate about storytelling, visual content, and digital trends, with the ability to create content that informs, entertains, and converts.


Key Responsibilities


	Create engaging photo, video, and written content for social media and digital platforms.
	Plan and execute a monthly content calendar aligned with marketing goals.
	Write compelling captions, scripts, blogs, and marketing copy.
	Capture and edit high-quality videos and images for campaigns and brand storytelling.
	Stay updated on social media trends and create timely, relevant content.
	Collaborate with the marketing team to execute campaigns and product promotions.
	Monitor content performance and recommend improvements based on analytics.
	Ensure all content aligns with the brand's voice, identity, and messaging.
	Cover company events, projects, and behind-the-scenes activities for digital platforms.


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree or equivalent in Mass Communication, Marketing, Media, or a related field.
	1–3 years of proven experience as a Content Creator or Social Media Content Specialist.
	Strong photography, videography, and video editing skills.
	Proficiency in CapCut, Canva, Adobe Premiere Pro, Photoshop, or similar creative tools.
	Excellent copywriting and storytelling abilities.
	Solid understanding of Instagram, Facebook, TikTok, LinkedIn, and YouTube content strategies.
	Creative mindset with attention to detail and strong organizational skills.
	Ability to  work independently and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.


Preferred Skills:


	Experience creating content for lifestyle, real estate, hospitality, or corporate brands.
	Basic motion graphics or animation skills.
	Familiarity with social media analytics and content performance metrics.


Salary
N170,000 - N180,000 Monthly. 

Application Closing Date: 7th July, 2026.

Click here to apply online

     

    

        Click here to Apply

Inventory Officer Job at Reo Havana Rooftop




        
    

        
        Reo Havana Rooftop is a vibrant, budget-friendly rooftop lounge, bar, and grill located at No. 32, 1st Avenue, Gwarinpa, Abuja (right beside Crumbles Bakery). It is highly regarded as a casual, minimalist hangout spot offering scenic views of the area alongside an aesthetically pleasing indoor dining section.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Inventory Officer

Job Location: Gwarinpa, Abuja (FCT)

Requirements
     

            
    




	Minimum of 1 year experience in inventory management in a restaurant, hotel or similar environment
	Strong knowdedge of inventory control procedures and stock management
	Ability to source quality supplies at the best prices
	Proficient in using inventory/ procurement software (advantage)
	Excellent organizational, analytical and negotiation skills
	Attention to detail and accuracy
	Self-motivated, reliable and able to work independently
	Must reside in Gwarinpa or environs.


What We Offer


	Competitive Salary.
	Transport Allowance.
	Daily Staff Lunch.
	Growth & Development Opportunities.
	Positive Work Environment.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
	Must reside in Gwarinpa or environs.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

Job Location: Iyana-Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Secretary to the Managing Director will provide high-level administrative and secretarial support to the MD, ensuring efficient management of schedules, correspondence, meetings and confidential information.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the MD&#39;s calendar, appointments and travel arrangements.
	Prepare and manage correspondence, reports, presentations and meeting minutes.
	Coordinate meetings, conferences and official engagements.
	Handle incoming calls, emails and visitors professionally.
	Maintain confidential records and company documents.
	Follow up on assignments and action points on behalf of the MD.
	Organize and maintain filing systems both electronic and physical.
	Liaise with internal departments and external stakeholders as directed by the MD.
	Must be 30 years and above


Requirements

	5-6 years of experience in a similar secretarial or administrative role.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion.
	Professional appearance and conduct.


Salary
N200,000 &ndash; N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: sule.e@martglobal.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.",

Executive Secretary to the Managing Director Job at MartGlobal Limited




        
    

        
        MartGlobal Limited - We are a reputable and fast-growing polythene manufacturing company committed to excellence in the production and supply of quality packaging solutions. We are seeking for a MATURE, highly organized, discreet and proactive professional to fill the position below:

Job Position: Executive Secretary to the Managing Director

Job Location: Iyana-Ipaja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Secretary to the Managing Director will provide high-level administrative and secretarial support to the MD, ensuring efficient management of schedules, correspondence, meetings and confidential information.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage the MD's calendar, appointments and travel arrangements.
	Prepare and manage correspondence, reports, presentations and meeting minutes.
	Coordinate meetings, conferences and official engagements.
	Handle incoming calls, emails and visitors professionally.
	Maintain confidential records and company documents.
	Follow up on assignments and action points on behalf of the MD.
	Organize and maintain filing systems both electronic and physical.
	Liaise with internal departments and external stakeholders as directed by the MD.
	Must be 30 years and above


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in a related field.
	5-6 years of experience in a similar secretarial or administrative role.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Ability to handle confidential information with discretion.
	Professional appearance and conduct.


Salary
N200,000 – N250,000 per month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their application to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
